name: MiniMax M2.7
license: MiniMax Model Community License (non-commercial use restrictions; commercial use requires separate agreement) — verify exact license text at https://huggingface.co/MiniMaxAI/MiniMax-M2.7/blob/main/LICENSE
archDisplay: Sparse Mixture-of-Experts, 256 local experts (8 active/token), 62 layers, RoPE + QK-RMSNorm attention; 230B total / 10B active params (~4.3% activation)
note: Agentic coding/reasoning model billed by MiniMax as its first model to autonomously drive a meaningful share of its own RL research/development workflow ("self-evolution"). Open weights on Hugging Face; also served via a 2x-priced 'M2.7-highspeed' deployment tier (same weights, faster serving) which is not modeled as a separate entry here.

results.notes: Reported by MiniMax as 'SWE-Pro' score in the official M2.7 launch post.
Reported by MiniMax as 'Terminal Bench 2' score in the official M2.7 launch post.

GDPval-AA Elo score across 45 evaluated models, stated on MiniMax's official M2.7 launch post as the highest among open-weight models (behind only Opus 4.6/Sonnet 4.6/GPT-5.4). Corroborated verbatim by independent press (MarkTechPost, Artificial Analysis).

MiniMax's official 'The MiniMax-M2 Series' technical report (arXiv:2605.26494), Table 4 full cross-model benchmark comparison, row 'AIME 2026' — M2.7 scores 94.2 vs M2.5's 87.2, and vs Opus 4.6 (92.5), Sonnet 4.6 (92.7), GPT-5.4 (97.0), Gemini 3.1 Pro (88.7). Also restated in the paper's abstract/intro prose ('M2.7 posts 94.2 on AIME 2026'). Mapped to the generic taxonomy 'aime' slug since the corpus has no year-specific AIME variant; the exact competition-year label used by MiniMax was 'AIME 2026'.

Official MiniMax-M2 technical report (arXiv:2605.26494), Table 4, row 'GPQA-Diamond' — M2.7 scores 89.8 (M2.5: 85.2; Opus 4.6: 89.6; Sonnet 4.6: 87.5; GPT-5.4: 92.0; Gemini 3.1 Pro: 94.1). Also called out in the report's intro prose alongside the AIME 2026 figure.

Official MiniMax-M2 technical report (arXiv:2605.26494), Table 4, row 'HLE' (Humanity's Last Exam) — M2.7 scores 28.0 (M2.5: 19.0; Opus 4.6: 36.7; Sonnet 4.6: 30.0; GPT-5.4: 41.6; Gemini 3.1 Pro: 44.7). Independently, Artificial Analysis's own HLE measurement for MiniMax-M2.7 (artificialanalysis.ai/models/minimax-m2-7) reads ~28%, closely corroborating this self-reported figure.

Official MiniMax-M2 technical report (arXiv:2605.26494), Table 4, row 'IFBench' — M2.7 scores 76.0 (M2.5: 72.0; Opus 4.6: 53.1; Sonnet 4.6: 56.6; GPT-5.4: 73.9; Gemini 3.1 Pro: 77.1). The report highlights this as a standout result, attributing it to the multi-domain RL strategy and rubric-based response filtering described in its Sections 4.3/6. Independently corroborated at ~76% by Artificial Analysis's own IFBench measurement (artificialanalysis.ai/models/minimax-m2-7).

Official MiniMax-M2 technical report (arXiv:2605.26494), Table 4, row 'MMLU-Pro' — M2.7 scores 81.8 (M2.5: 85.2 [sic, M2.5 scores higher here per the published table]; Opus 4.6: 89.1; Sonnet 4.6: 87.3; GPT-5.4: 87.5; Gemini 3.1 Pro: 91.2).

Official MiniMax-M2 technical report (arXiv:2605.26494), Table 4, 'Search' block, row 'BrowseComp' — M2.7 scores 77.8 (M2.5: 76.3; Opus 4.6: 84.0; Sonnet 4.6: 74.7; GPT-5.4: 82.7; Gemini 3.1 Pro: 85.9). Distinct from the report's separate 'BrowseComp-zh' (Chinese-language variant) figure used only in an earlier architecture-ablation table (Table 3), not in this final-model cross-comparison.

Second, independently-run GPQA Diamond measurement by Artificial Analysis (their own eval harness), distinct from MiniMax's self-reported 89.8 already added above from arXiv:2605.26494 Table 4 — different methodology/harness, hence recorded as a separate result under the same gpqa slug (mirroring how this corpus already dual-sources e.g. Qwen3.6-Plus's GPQA and Grok 4.5's Terminal-Bench). Read directly off AA's live model-comparison chart for MiniMax-M2.7 (rank-ordered list cross-referenced against adjacent score list) on 2026-07-14.

Second, independently-run Terminal-Bench v2.1 measurement by Artificial Analysis (their own harness/sandbox), distinct from MiniMax's self-reported 57.0 already present in this model's corpus entry (sourced from minimax.io's launch post) and from the 57.0 in arXiv:2605.26494 Table 4 — recorded as a separate result under the same terminal-bench-2 slug per this corpus's existing dual-sourcing convention (e.g. Grok 4.5's terminal-bench-2 has both a self-reported and an AA-independent entry). Read off AA's live model-comparison chart for MiniMax-M2.7 on 2026-07-14.
Agents' Last Exam (ALE-CLI, Overall leaderboard), OpenClaw harness, High reasoning effort: Pass rate 5.9% / Score 14.2% (rank 49). Score component used per corpus convention.

verificationNotes:
Confirmed released (not just announced) via official minimax.io news post, MiniMax API docs, NVIDIA developer blog, Hugging Face model page, and Artificial Analysis independent tracker — all agree on a March 18, 2026 launch. Param counts (230B total/10B active) corroborated by both NVIDIA's technical blog and the official model page; the HF repo metadata itself lists a slightly different '229B' which is very likely a rounding artifact of the same figure. Context window cited as 200K by NVIDIA and ~205K tokens by Artificial Analysis (likely 204,800 rounded two different ways) — used 200 as the vendor-stated round figure.

2026-07-16: Researched the six core-taxonomy gaps (mmlu, math, humaneval, livecodebench, swe-bench, mmmu) for MiniMax M2.7 across (1) MiniMax's own materials — minimax.io/news/minimax-m27-en, minimax.io/models/text/m27, github.com/MiniMax-AI/MiniMax-M2.7 README, and the HF model card — and, most authoritatively, the official technical report (arXiv:2605.26494, "The MiniMax-M2 Series"), whose Table 4 is explicitly the paper's full cross-model benchmark comparison for M2.7 vs Opus 4.6/Sonnet 4.6/GPT-5.4/Gemini 3.1 Pro (with "–" reserved for scores a model "has not reported... or has not yet released"); (2) independent trackers — Artificial Analysis's MiniMax-M2.7 model page (Intelligence Index v4.1, which substitutes GDPval-AA v2/tau3-Banking/Terminal-Bench/SciCode/HLE/GPQA-D/CritPt/AA-Omniscience/AA-LCR for the legacy suite), Epoch AI's MiniMax-M2.7 page (composite Capabilities Index only, rank 52/211, no per-benchmark breakdown), and Vals.ai's SWE-bench Verified leaderboard (MiniMax is entirely absent from it); (3) sibling models MiniMax-M2 and MiniMax-M1 in this corpus, whose own self-reported livecodebench (83, 65) and swe-bench (69.4, 56) scores are for earlier, differently-evaluated checkpoints (Oct 2025 / Jun 2025) predating the M2.5/M2.7 suite redesign, so they cannot be carried forward as M2.7 evidence. None of these sources report mmlu, math (MATH-500), humaneval, livecodebench, or swe-bench (Verified) for M2.7 specifically — Table 4 shows these were dropped from the suite entirely in favor of mmlu-pro, aime (2026), swe-bench-pro/multilingual/multi-swe-bench, and scicode, not run-and-withheld. This corpus's own benchmark catalog corroborates the saturation rationale (mmlu: "Largely saturated at the frontier"; humaneval: "Long-saturated at the frontier"). A widely-mirrored secondary-blog claim (wavespeed.ai, thomas-wiegold.com) of an unsourced "SWE-bench Verified 78%" figure for M2.7 was checked and rejected as uncorroborated: it appears in none of the primary sources above nor on Vals.ai, and looks like an SEO-content-mill artifact rather than a real measurement. mmmu is a multimodal/vision benchmark and M2.7 is a text-only model (modalities: ["text"], capabilities.vision: false), so it is architecturally inapplicable rather than merely unpublished. All six gaps resolved as omitted_no_evidence with no changes to the existing results array.
